content:
I saw Alice comment:

This build assert does not depend on runtime values, so it can be written as a const block:

const {
    assert!(OFFSET < N, "Index out of bounds when accessing CoherentArray");
}

Could we check in klint if the expression asserted on in build_assert! contains non-static values? If not issue a warning to use a const assert instead?

This is something that I have already planned in https://github.com/Rust-for-Linux/klint/issues/7. The title doesn't sound its relevant, but as part of that implementation I need to track all values that flow into a build_assert! call
and mark as effectively constant (you only want to emit warning if build_assert! makes use of a non-static value and didn't mark it as #[inline(always)].
oh interesting, I think it should be a separate diagnostic message, but it sounds like you're going to do that :)
